title: Unable to build multi architecture image
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
I'm trying to build multi-arch images inside my CICD for my needs using a container agent on my kubernetes cluster.
The building of AMD image works well but I can't build ARM images if the pod is running on an x86_64 system

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Run an x86_64 ubuntu:22.04 image / OS
2. Build and tag the image with platform flag set to linux/arm64. cf Dockerfile : 
```
buildah build --platform linux/arm64 -t mytag:latest .
```
```
FROM ubuntu:22.04
RUN apt-get update
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[49/49] STEP 1/2: FROM ubuntu:22.04
Resolved "ubuntu" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:22.04...
Getting image source signatures
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[49/49] STEP 2/2: RUN apt-get update
process failed to start with error: fork/exec /bin/sh: exec format errorprocess exited with error: exec: not startedsubprocess exited with status 1
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
Build without any issue

**Output of `apt list buildah`:**

```
Listing... Done
buildah/unknown,now 1.30.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs41.25 amd64 [installed]
buildah/unknown 1.30.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs41.25 arm64
buildah/unknown 1.30.0-0ubuntu22.04+obs41.23 s390x
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.30.0
Go Version:      go1.18.1
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.1.0-rc.1
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.25.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=22.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=jammy
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS"
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="22.04"
VERSION="22.04.2 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)"
VERSION_CODENAME=jammy
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=jammy
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux ccita-6477038d08f7c671110c25aa-0-sl0kvet4 5.10.178-162.673.amzn2.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Apr 24 23:34:06 UTC 2023 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
cat: /etc/containers/storage.conf: No such file or directory
```

comments.body: Hi @afreyermuth98 , for this you will `qemu-user-static` on your host.

Hey @flouthoc thank you for your answer but I already have it installed :/
Just before my build command :
```
qemu-user-static is already the newest version (1:6.2+dfsg-2ubuntu6.8).
```

Same issue in rootless buildah running in kubernetes
Native architecture builded fine, arm64 fails with error
```
$ buildah -v
buildah version 1.23.1 (image-spec 1.0.1, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)

$ dpkg -l qemu*
Desired=Unknown/Install/Remove/Purge/Hold
| Status=Not/Inst/Conf-files/Unpacked/halF-conf/Half-inst/trig-aWait/Trig-pend
|/ Err?=(none)/Reinst-required (Status,Err: uppercase=bad)
||/ Name             Version               Architecture Description
+++-================-=====================-============-==================================================
un  qemu-user-binfmt <none>                <none>       (no description available)
ii  qemu-user-static 1:6.2+dfsg-2ubuntu6.9 amd64        QEMU user mode emulation binaries (static version)


$ echo $_BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS

$ echo $BUILDAH_ISOLATION
chroot
$ echo $STORAGE_DRIVER
vfs


$cat Dockerfile 
FROM node:18-alpine
WORKDIR /app
RUN yarn install --production
CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]

$ buildah  build --arch arm64 
STEP 1/4: FROM node:18-alpine
Resolved "node" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/node:18-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob edee738dcba1 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 8c6d1654570f [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob 206c2bf091e9 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob e8098206ab59 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 0cce238340 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/4: WORKDIR /app
STEP 3/4: RUN yarn install --production
process exited with error: fork/exec /bin/sh: exec format errorsubprocess exited with status 1
error building at STEP "RUN yarn install --production": exit status 1
ERRO[0002] exit status 1
```

Without --arch arg works fine
```
$ buildah  build 
STEP 1/4: FROM node:18-alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/node:18-alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ee16df044bfc done  
Copying blob c017600940c6 done  
Copying blob c9f8586f07bd done  
Copying blob 31e352740f53 done  
Copying config 6dcce61929 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/4: WORKDIR /app
STEP 3/4: RUN yarn install --production
yarn install v1.22.19
info No lockfile found.
[1/4] Resolving packages...
[2/4] Fetching packages...
[3/4] Linking dependencies...
[4/4] Building fresh packages...
success Saved lockfile.
Done in 0.06s.
STEP 4/4: CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]
COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 78a822fe2a2d skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 4f4b74c286d2 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob 04c82a4955a1 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 370f4ccb18d7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 8089d4008e58 done  
Copying config 4cf52cfde6 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 4cf52cfde6a
4cf52cfde6ae8a32b801f3cab1c2d7608cb61aab09f48b5e955bd3a61ce805aa
```
This looks like an issue with QEMU-User-static not installed or broken.

@rhatdan thanks for your response, it is point me to right direction
In my case issue was in not registered QEMU in system (package installed, but system doesn't know how to use it).
Possible issue - missed binfmt_misc on the host system

Example
arm64 file recognized
```
file /bin/arm64test 
/bin/arm64test: ELF 64-bit LSB executable, ARM aarch64, version 1 (SYSV), statically linked, stripped
```
but OS can't run it with QEMU
```
arm64test 
bash: /usr/bin/arm64test: cannot execute binary file: Exec format error
```

But QEMU emulator works fine
```
qemu-aarch64-static /bin/arm64test

Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.

To generate this message, Docker took the following steps:
 1. The Docker client contacted the Docker daemon.
 2. The Docker daemon pulled the "hello-world" image from the Docker Hub.
    (arm64v8)
 3. The Docker daemon created a new container from that image which runs the
    executable that produces the output you are currently reading.
 4. The Docker daemon streamed that output to the Docker client, which sent it
    to your terminal.

To try something more ambitious, you can run an Ubuntu container with:
 $ docker run -it ubuntu bash

Share images, automate workflows, and more with a free Docker ID:
 https://hub.docker.com/

For more examples and ideas, visit:
 https://docs.docker.com/get-started/
```


I fixed it with registering QEMU on kubernetes nodes like here https://l0w.dev/posts/qemu-user-static-on-kubernetes/
Now all work
```
 arm64test 

Hello from Docker!
This message shows that your installation appears to be working correctly.

To generate this message, Docker took the following steps:
 1. The Docker client contacted the Docker daemon.
 2. The Docker daemon pulled the "hello-world" image from the Docker Hub.
    (arm64v8)
 3. The Docker daemon created a new container from that image which runs the
    executable that produces the output you are currently reading.
 4. The Docker daemon streamed that output to the Docker client, which sent it
    to your terminal.

To try something more ambitious, you can run an Ubuntu container with:
 $ docker run -it ubuntu bash

Share images, automate workflows, and more with a free Docker ID:
 https://hub.docker.com/

For more examples and ideas, visit:
 https://docs.docker.com/get-started/
```

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
